method: Two public-record sweeps (2026-07-21): government releases, state budget documents, PIB/Parliament-reply reports, mainstream business press. Disbursals distinguished from approvals/LoCs/MoUs; ARREARS recorded as the key performance signal; 'UNVERIFIED' and absence-of-published-data recorded as findings.
headline_findings: ARREARS DIVIDE: Telangana Rs 3,736cr confirmed arrears (overlapping counts to >Rs 4,500cr since 2016/17); AP Rs 3,000-5,000cr pending (YSRCP-era disbursal ~Rs 2,000cr/5y); Punjab's 'Rs 150cr Q1 FY26 exceeded prior two years combined' is an implicit arrears admission; J&K NCSS approved ~Rs 83cr in FY24 against Rs 28,400cr outlay with documented industry delay complaints.

TRANSPARENCY DIVIDE: Rajasthan press-releases annual RIPS disbursals (Rs 765.78cr FY25, +293%); Gujarat publishes MSME-track numbers (Rs 7,864cr/5y) but gates the Large/Mega dashboard behind login; Maharashtra and Karnataka publish NO annual disbursal series; Haryana and West Bengal have no findable aggregate outgo.

OUTLIERS: MP actively disburses on-stage (Rs 270cr+425cr at one conclave; claims liabilities cleared); Odisha budgets only Rs 15cr/yr for IPR subsidies against a Rs 24,823cr MoU pipeline; Assam's semiconductor state share is Rs 3,789cr of the Rs 14,044cr Tata OSAT stack.

funds_evidence.fact: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat scheme (launched 2022) has provided assistance to enterprises since inception (MSME track)
Total MSME industrial assistance disbursed by Gujarat govt FY2020-21 to FY2024-25 (capital subsidy, interest subvention, credit guarantee across policy frameworks)

Legacy scheme disbursals still running in parallel: Industrial Policy 2020 claims Rs 345 crore across 8,700 claims; Industrial Policy 2015 claims Rs 137 crore across 4,400 claims (1 Apr 2024 - 8 Jan 2025 window)
IFP (Investor Facilitation Portal) has an 'Incentives Granted Dashboard' menu item, but no numbers are publicly visible without login - Large/Mega-track disbursal totals UNVERIFIED from public web

1,948 NCSS claims disbursed to date (1,139 Jammu + 809 Kashmir divisions); 2,346 industrial establishments applied, 918 granted registration (figures from Rising Kashmir reporting; Rs 31.76 crore figure in same coverage relates to pharma-sector releases/29 pharma units per Rajya Sabha reply by MoS Chemicals & Fertilizers Anupriya Patel — possible conflation in secondary reporting, treat the Rs 31.76 cr as pharma-specific)

method_notes: ~21 web searches/fetches performed 2026-07-21; evidence restricted to government releases, budget documents/analyses, and mainstream Indian business press

Disbursal (money actually paid out) was distinguished from approvals/LoCs/MoUs (commitments): Rajasthan, Gujarat-MSME and UP have hard disbursal numbers; TN and Maharashtra evidence is dominated by budget allocations and approval/MoU pipelines; Karnataka only has a 3-year aggregate of 'incentives offered'

TN's Rs 2,420 cr Investment Promotion Incentive figure comes from secondary budget coverage (franchisebhoomi.com summarising the FY26 budget) - the finance dept demand-for-grants PDF was not parsed line-by-line; treat as reported-but-secondary
No CAG state-audit paragraphs on incentive outgo surfaced within the fetch budget for any of the six states - a targeted cag.gov.in search would be the next step
Karnataka Deccan Herald article date could not be confirmed (page 403-blocked); search metadata suggests mid-2026

Absence findings: neither Maharashtra (PSI outgo line) nor Karnataka (annual disbursal series) publishes an accessible yearly incentive-disbursal figure - this opacity is itself a result; contrast with Rajasthan which press-releases its annual RIPS disbursal

22 web searches/fetches total. Direct WebFetch verification performed on 3 key sources: Deccan Chronicle (Telangana Rs 3,736 cr arrears breakdown), KNN India (Telangana Rs 400-500 cr March-2025 tranche), Hans India (AP Rs 3,000-5,000 cr pending, minister on record, 2025-04-05).

Evidence hierarchy applied: J&K figures trace to Lok Sabha/Rajya Sabha written replies but were obtained via PIB release (PRID 2042127) and news reports of the replies, NOT the raw sansad.in PQ PDFs — figures should be re-confirmed against the PQ PDFs before publication.

Figures marked '(from search snippet)' or with UNVERIFIED dates came from search-engine summaries of news articles that were not individually fetched; the headline figures are consistent across multiple outlets but exact article dates were not captured.

Telangana arrears figures vary by cutoff: Rs 3,736 cr (as of Dec 2023, industries dept), Rs 4,250 cr (since 2016, 2025 reporting), >Rs 4,500 cr (since 2017, SC/ST-focused reporting incl. Rs 2,200 cr SC/ST component). These are overlapping counts, not additive.

Arrears-as-signal summary: Telangana and AP have explicit multi-thousand-crore confirmed arrears; Punjab's own 'Rs 150 cr in Q1 exceeded prior two years combined' is an implicit arrears admission; J&K NCSS shows ~Rs 83 cr/yr approvals against a Rs 28,400 cr outlay plus documented delay complaints; MP is the outlier actively disbursing on-stage (Rs 270+425 cr at one conclave) and claiming liabilities cleared; Odisha budgets only Rs 15 cr/yr for IPR subsidies against a Rs 24,823 cr MoU pipeline.
access_issues: invest.up.gov.in PDFs fail TLS certificate verification for the fetcher ('unable to verify the first certificate') - figures taken from search-index snippets of the official press releases; URLs load fine in a normal browser
business-standard.com and deccanherald.com and thestatesman.com return HTTP 403 to the fetcher (bot-blocking) - details captured from search-result summaries only
Gujarat IFP portal 'Incentives Granted Dashboard' is behind login; public landing page shows menu links but no numbers

PRS Maharashtra budget analysis page does not itemise the industrial promotion subsidy line; the full Economic Survey of Maharashtra 2024-25 PDF (cdnbbsr.s3waas.gov.in) was not parsed due to size/fetch budget

MPIDC sanction orders: invest.mp.gov.in/notifications-orders/ confirmed public, but counting/totalling individual sanction orders requires paginated portal scraping not performed in this sweep — the conclave disbursal figures (Rs 270 cr/288 MSMEs, Rs 425 cr/140 large units, Rs 360 cr/900 units) are the best public proxy.
sansad.in PQ PDFs not directly fetched for NCSS/UNNATI state-wise numbers; relied on PIB + news reports of the Parliament replies.

West Bengal: no consolidated MSME incentive outgo (actual disbursal) figure exists in easily-searchable public record; only budget allocations (Rs 1,229 cr BE 2025-26) found. WB detailed demand-for-grants PDFs would be needed.
Haryana: no HEEP-2020 aggregate disbursal or pendency figure found anywhere public; CAG state finance audit for Haryana not checked directly.
Punjab Rs 150 cr Q1 FY2025-26 figure appeared identically in two independent search summaries but no primary URL was captured — attribution UNVERIFIED.
Possible conflation in Rising Kashmir coverage between Rs 31.76 cr pharma-sector NCSS releases and total FY2025-26 NCSS disbursals — flagged rather than resolved.
No CAG audit paras specifically on state industrial-incentive arrears were surfaced within the fetch budget for any of the 9 states.
